prompt: Name Canada's capital and the ocean along its eastern seaboard. Reply as City; Ocean.
In two or three sentences, explain why hash tables typically offer O(1) average-time lookups.
Which major river runs through Cairo? Respond with only the river's name.
What does DNS mean, and why do networks rely on it? Keep your answer to one sentence.
A factory inventory has 720 units. In the morning, 15% are dispatched. Later, 95 more units ship out. How many units remain? Answer with only the final number.
An appliance costs $890.00. It receives a 22% discount, then a $50 off coupon is applied to the reduced price. What is the final price? Answer with only the number, rounded to two decimal places.
A startup had 2,500 subscribers in Q1 and grows 12% quarter over quarter. How many subscribers after 2 quarters? Answer rounded to the nearest whole number.
Calculate: what is 144 / 12 + 7 * 3? Reply with only the final number.
Label this product review as Positive, Negative, Neutral, or Mixed: Absolutely thrilled with the camera quality and battery endurance!
Determine the sentiment (Positive/Negative/Neutral/Mixed): The checkout process was confusing and support never replied to my emails.
Classify sentiment as Positive, Negative, Neutral, or Mixed, including a one-sentence justification: The hotel breakfast was excellent, however our room faced a noisy construction site all night.

Condense the passage below into a single sentence of at most 25 words: Cloud-native architectures rely on containers, microservices, and automated orchestration to improve scalability, resilience, and deployment speed across distributed systems.

Provide exactly three bullet points (max 12 words each) summarizing: Regular exercise strengthens the heart and lungs. It also reduces stress hormones and improves sleep quality. Over time, consistent activity lowers the risk of chronic diseases like diabetes.
Summarize in one sentence: Quantum computing leverages superposition and entanglement to explore many computational paths simultaneously, promising breakthroughs in cryptography and drug discovery.
List every named entity with its type as JSON [{"text":..., "type":...}]: Tim Cook unveiled the iPhone 17 at Apple Park in Cupertino during September 2025.
Return a JSON array of entities (text, type) found in: Elena Rodriguez moved to Spotify headquarters in Stockholm this February with her manager Omar Hassan.
Extract named entities as JSON with keys text and type from: Google DeepMind published new research in London last November.
Alice, Bob, and Carol each prefer a different color: red, blue, green. Alice does not like green. Bob likes blue. Which person prefers red? Answer with only the name.

Priya, Devon, and Casey finished a cycling race in first, second, and third place. Priya did not finish first. Devon finished before Casey. Casey did not finish third. State placements as Name-Place, Name-Place, Name-Place.

Four colleagues—Nina, Omar, Priya, and Quinn—each drive a different vehicle: sedan, truck, van, motorcycle. Nina does not drive the sedan. Omar drives neither the van nor the sedan. Quinn drives the motorcycle. Who drives the sedan? Answer with only the name.
Write a Python function count_vowels(text) that returns the number of vowels (a,e,i,o,u) in text, case-insensitive. Return only runnable code, no explanation.
Implement is_palindrome(text) in Python, ignoring punctuation, spaces, and letter casing. Return only runnable code.
Create a Python function merge_intervals(intervals) that merges overlapping intervals and returns them sorted by start. Code only, no commentary.
Implement second_largest(nums) in Python to return the second-largest value, treating duplicates correctly (e.g. [5,5,3] -> 3). Return only runnable code.
What is the tallest mountain on Earth? Reply with only the mountain name.
Classify the sentiment as Positive, Negative, Neutral, or Mixed: The documentation is adequate and the API endpoints are clearly described.
